sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (he) + nhặt (pick up) + sự (thing) + trên (on) + sàn (floor)
Chị (she) + cất (put away) + sự (thing) + vào (into) + hộp (box)
notes_md:
**sự** is not really “thing” in everyday Vietnamese. It’s a formal word used to turn verbs or adjectives into abstract nouns (like “-ness” or “the act of”).

You rarely use **sự** alone — it almost always sits in front of another word to create a concept.  

It often appears in more formal or written language, especially in set phrases.  
Close in meaning to **việc**, but **việc** is more concrete (“a task/job”), while **sự** feels abstract (“a concept/state”).
exampleSentencesTranslated: He picks up the thing on the floor.
She puts the thing into the box.
I give the thing to you.

definitions.vi: Từ dùng để tạo danh từ trừu tượng, chỉ sự việc, tính chất hoặc trạng thái.
definitions.en: A word used to form abstract nouns indicating an event, quality, or state.

example.en: Patience is very important in life.
example.vi: Sự kiên nhẫn rất quan trọng trong cuộc sống.

translations.en: the (state of) / the act of / -ness / -tion (nominalizing prefix)
